source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the turns ratio of the primary to secondary coils of an ideal transformer is 44:9. Point b is a tap on the primary coil and is exactly at the center of the primary coil. S is a single-pole double-throw switch, and the load resistance is \( R \). All meters are ideal. A sinusoidal alternating current is applied across the c and d terminals of the primary coil, as shown in the figure. Which of the following statements is correct?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Regardless of whether S is connected to a or b, the direction of current through the resistor \( R \) changes 100 times in 1 second

choice_map.A: When S is connected to a, the ammeter reading is 0
choice_map.B: Regardless of whether S is connected to a or b, the direction of current through the resistor \( R \) changes 100 times in 1 second
choice_map.C: When S is switched from a to b, the voltmeter reading will be halved
choice_map.D: When S is switched from a to b, the power consumed by resistor \( R \) will double

extraction_notes: Removed the leading hint/instruction text and extracted only the question body.

image_dependency: question_text begins with 'As shown in the figure', indicating the diagram is part of the problem statement
The setup references spatial/structural elements in the diagram such as terminals c and d, point b as a center tap on the primary coil, and switch positions a/b
multi_step: Solving requires reasoning about transformer turns ratio 44:9 and the effect of switching between a full primary connection and a center tap
The options involve different electrical quantities and behaviors: ammeter reading, voltmeter reading, current direction change frequency, and resistor power
The problem combines circuit interpretation from the figure with AC/transformer behavior, which is more than a single direct lookup
verifiability: This is a 4-choice multiple-choice question with a single provided answer_text matching one option exactly
The answer claim is specific and checkable: current direction through R changes 100 times in 1 second
The circuit assumptions are explicit ('ideal transformer', 'all meters are ideal'), which supports rule-based or physics-based checking
